entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: This course gives students a glimpse into how the ideas that guide our study of geometry were first discovered.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: This course is intended as a precursor to Part 2; however, the order may be reversed to facilitate combining students.

rows.label: Story of Geometry: Part 1
rows.scheduleInfo:
Story of Geometry: Part 1
1 time/week 15 min
rows.books: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow||The Student Bible Atlas, Revised Edition||Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

books.title: The Student Bible Atlas, Revised Edition
Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.

blocks.text: Optional: Use a picture, map, or engaging question to prepare the student to receive the idea from the day’s lesson. Talk about one or two unknown words that will be crucial to understanding.
Read the new passage out loud or have the student read it orally or silently.
Have the student narrate orally or in writing.
• Give students an opportunity to share their thoughts on the lesson and any connections made.
Optional: Attempt to recreate some of the problems from the stories.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Introduction
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ INTRO
As you begin your study of geometry, this book will help you understand some concepts better by showing you how they were discovered and used to solve real problems.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Prologue" p.7-10 (UK 7-9) "The string, the" - "and the shadow."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
The Natural Sense of Order
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.1 p.12-13 (UK 12-13) "Geometry itself" - "built into them."

• Look around the room in which you are sitting or outside. Write a paragraph telling what elements of mathematics (including geometry) you see.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 3
lessons.body:
Mathematics as Beauty 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.1 p.13-14 (UK 13-14) "Within ourselves" - "geometry really began."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 4
lessons.body:
From Fear to Wonder
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.2 p.15-16 (UK 15-16) "When and how did" - "an amazing surprise."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
You may wish to preview p.15 which talks about the age of the earth.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 5
lessons.body:
Structure in Nature 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.1 p.17-18 (UK 16-17) "Before we learn" - "of living things."

• How could seeing basic geometric shapes help you with nature study?
• Describe an object in terms of its shape. Use geometry words such as line, curve, spiral, perpendicular, etc.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 6
lessons.body:
Geometry of Living Things
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.2 p.18-19 (UK 17-18) "Did you ever" - "five-pointed star."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 7
lessons.body:
From the Art Gallery of the Universe
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.2 p.19-20 (UK 18-19) "Best of all" - "dim Stone Age."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 8
lessons.body:
Technics and Art
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.3 p.21-22 (UK 20-21) "Stone Age men" - "variety of nature's forms."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Please note the illustration of the partially nude woman on p. 23.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 9
lessons.body:
Design Mimics Nature
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.3 p.22-24 (UK 21-22) "Perhaps unconsciously" - "using circles."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Please note the illustration of the partially nude woman on p. 23.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 10
lessons.body:
⬚ 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 10
Circles
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ INTRO
Where in this room do you see circles?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.3 p.24-25 (UK 22) "Today we are" - "they gave up!"

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 11
lessons.body:
Drawing Perfect Circle with String
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ INTRO
How would you define an "accurate" circle?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.3 p.26-27 (UK 22-24) "So, you see" - "the Stone Age."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 12
lessons.body:
Exam Week
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 13
lessons.body:
Agrarian Society
Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow, Student Bible Atlas

➜ INTRO
This section is titled "Reading the Shadows." What do you think that means?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.4 p.29-30 (UK 26-27) "Here our story" - "of the shadows."

• This map shows the Fertile Crescent. What do you notice about the surrounding land?
⍞ Map Resource: Student Bible Atlas "Map 1: Lands of the Bible" p.4-5

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 14
lessons.body:
Noticing Shadows 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.4 p.30-32 (UK 27-29) "Perhaps it was" - "a repeated pattern."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 15
lessons.body:
Measuring Time and Seasons
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.4 p.32-35 (UK 29-31) "The sun seemed" - "the fruitful year."

• Look at a sundial. How is it similar to, and different from, a clock?
∞ Image Link: Sundial

➜ OBSERVE
Choose one of the ways to observe shadows from the book to try yourself.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 16
lessons.body:
Usefulness of Right Angles
Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ INTRO
A surveyor is someone who makes very precise measurements of land to determine property boundaries. Why do you think that might be important? Look at the picture of a modern surveyor. This story will show how this profession got its start.
∞ Image Link: Surveyor

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.5 p.36-38 (UK 32-34) "Now at last" - "on the earth?"
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
Village of Jarmo: First Evidence of Straight Lines & Right Angles (c.5000 B.C., Syria)

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 17
lessons.body:
The 3-4-5 Right Triangle 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What right angles did you notice last week?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.5 p.38-41 (UK 34-37) "We know part" - "perfect right angle."

➜ PRACTICE
Using your geometry tools, try to construct a 3-4-5 right triangle.
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
Rope Stretchers (First Surveyors; c.3000 B.C., Egypt)

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 18
lessons.body:
The Plumb-Line
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
Tell about a perfect right triangle.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.5 p.42-44 (UK 37-39) "Now that surveyors" - "way to keep peace."

• Where are levels and plumb lines important today?

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 19
lessons.body:
Area and Architecture
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.5 p.44-46 (UK 39-41) "Again the idea" - "of the temple."
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
Oldest Man Made Stone Structure (Step-Pyramid at Sakkara, c.2750 B.C.)
Great Pyramid (c.2850 B.C.)

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 20
lessons.body:
Early Astronomy
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.6 p.47-50 (UK 42-44) "Meantime on the" - "along the Nile."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 21
lessons.body:
Arcs
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.6 p.50-52 (UK 44-46) "The Egyptians" - "exactly six equal parts."

➜ PRACTICE
Using your geometry tools, try to construct a circle and divide it into six equal parts. Use the following pictures to help you.
⍞ Image Resource: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow p.52-53

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 22
lessons.body:
Measuring Star Travel 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.6 p.52-54 (UK 46-48) "Anyhow, by chance" - "celestial bodies."

➜ PRACTICE
Into how many equal parts is the circle of a clock divided by the hour marks? Using the same method you used last week to construct a circle with equal parts, construct a clock face (hours only).
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
First Continuous Astronomical Data Started (747 B.C., Mesopotamia)

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 23
lessons.body:
Circles as Instruments of Measure
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ INTRO
Besides astronomy, in which fields of study or in what other endeavors do you think the circle could be used?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.6 p.54-57 (UK 48-51) "Apart from" - "Near Eastern geometry."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 24
lessons.body:
Exam Week
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 25
lessons.body:
Babylonians & Greeks
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow, Student Bible Atlas

➜ RECAP
What do you remember from last term?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.7 p.59-60 (UK 54-55) "At the start" - "the Black Sea."

➜ LOCATE 
⍞ Map Resource: Student Bible Atlas "Map 13: Babylon About 550 B.C." p.16
Look at the map of Babylon. Locate the Mediterranean and Black Seas (not labeled). Find Nineveh. What river runs by it? What Bible stories do you remember about Nineveh or King Nebuchadnezzar?
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
First Theoretical Geometry (c.6th century B.C., Ionia)
Thales of Miletus (c.624 B.C.-c.548 B.C.)

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 26
lessons.body:
From Practical to Theoretical Geometry 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow, Historical Atlas of the World

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ INTRO
⍞ Map Resource: Historical Atlas of the World "Classical Greece and Athenian Empire About 450 B.C." p.7 
Find the colony of Ionia. Look to the south to find the city of Miletus. This is where today's story takes place.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.7 p.60-61 (UK 55-57) "So the 6th century" - "first scientists."

• Tell how the location of Miletus influenced its knowledge and development.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 27
lessons.body:
Thales: Founder of Geometry 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.7 p.61-62 (UK 57) "The Greeks had" - "founder of geometry."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 28
lessons.body:
Thales and Scientific Inquiry 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.8 p.63-64 (UK 58-59) "Thales, the father" - "the next season."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 29
lessons.body:
Importance of Observation 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What was Thales studying in last week's lesson? What do you think he will do?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.8 p.65-67 (UK 59-61) "Now he made" - "under your feet."

• Tell one of the stories of Thales.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 30
lessons.body:
Practical + Theoretical = New Ideas 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.8 p.67-68 (UK 61-62) "Nobody likes" - "theoretical geometry."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 31
lessons.body:
Magic & Mathematics
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.9 p.69-70 (UK 63-64) "In the Land" - "was a magician."

➜ DISCUSS
How do you think Thales calculated the height of the Great Pyramid? Find something tall outside (a tree, telephone pole, etc.). Can you devise a way you could figure out its height?

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 32
lessons.body:
Proportion
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
Were you able to figure out how to calculate the height of the tall object you chose? 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.9 p.70-72 (UK 64-66) "To be sure" - "abstract proportion."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 33
lessons.body:
Shadows & Right Triangles 
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.9 p.73-75 (UK 66-67) "Where others saw" - "height of the pyramid."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 34
lessons.body:
Finding the Height of a Tall Object
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ INTRO
Today we can try Thales' feat ourselves.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.9 p.75-76 (UK 67-70) "Before you hear" - "height of the pyramid."

➜ PRACTICE
Use shadows and proportions to calculate the height of a tall object.

• Explain how Thales calculated the height of the pyramid.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 35
lessons.body:
The Abstract Right Triangle
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.9 p.76-78 (UK 70-71) "When he asked" - "of reasoning."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Geometry: Part 1 - Lesson 36
lessons.body:
Exam Week
⍞ Materials: String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
String, Straight-Edge, and Shadow
